QAComet LLC, a Colorado limited liability company ("QAComet," "we," or
"us") respects your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we
collect, use, and safeguard personal and non-personal information when
you use our website or engage with our services. By using our site, you
agree to the terms outlined below. If you do not agree, please do not
use our website.

QAComet is built on trust. Our clients rely on us to test sensitive
systems and advise on complex workflows. We take privacy seriously — and
this policy reflects our commitment to handling data transparently,
securely, and responsibly.
We never sell your personal information.

This Privacy Policy applies only to information collected
by QAComet through our website (
We collect two types of information:

. Information you voluntarily
provide, such as your name, email address, phone number, and
company details when you fill out a form, schedule a
consultation, or engage us for services.

. Technical data such as IP
address, browser type, device type, and pages visited. This may
be collected automatically via cookies or analytics tools.

. QAComet does not collect, store,

We process personal data only where we have a lawful basis, such

Each third-party service has its own privacy and security

A Data Processing Addendum (DPA) is available upon request for
clients subject to GDPR, HIPAA, or similar frameworks.

We retain information only as long
as necessary to provide services, comply with legal obligations, or
resolve disputes. Clients may request deletion of their data at any
time by contacting us. We comply promptly, unless data must be
retained for legal or operational reasons.

Our site may use cookies or similar technologies to
improve website performance. You can modify your browser settings to
refuse some or all cookies. However, disabling cookies may affect
website functionality. Some browsers offer a “Do Not Track” (DNT)
feature. QAComet does not currently respond to DNT signals because
there is no uniform standard governing their use or interpretation.

We take reasonable administrative and technical
measures to protect your information. This includes encrypted
storage (via Google Workspace), limited access controls, and
mandatory two-factor authentication for all internal accounts.
Despite our efforts, no method of transmission over the internet or
method of electronic storage is 100% secure. We cannot guarantee
absolute security.

Depending on your location, you may have rights
under laws like the GDPR (EU) or CCPA (California), including:
The right to access or correct your personal data
The right to request deletion of your data
The right to opt out of communications
To exercise any of these rights, contact us at:

Our site and services are not directed to
children under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal
information from children.If we become aware that we have collected
personal information from a child under 13 without verifiable
parental consent, we will take steps to delete that information
promptly.

We may update this Privacy Policy from
